NVDA.reasoning: NVDA does not present a valid swing breakout setup at this time. The breakout move has already occurred — the squeeze fired in a prior run (squeeze_off: true per wiki), driving price from the $164 swing low to a 52-week high of $216.83 on Apr 27. The BB width is already expanded at 0.2591 (daily) — volatility already expanded, the move is done. No fresh squeeze or coiling pattern is forming. Price is now consolidating in a tight hourly range: resistance at $214.73 (7 tests) and support at $208.20 (15 tests, volume confirmed), a range of $6.53. For a bullish breakout signal, I require: (1) a daily close above $216.83 (the 52-week high resistance, per wiki), AND (2) volume at 1.5x+ the 20-day average. Neither condition is met — daily relative volume is only 0.84x and hourly is 0.56x, both well below threshold. The 10-day hourly volume bias is DOWN, and hourly OBV trend is DOWN — no accumulation surge. Daily RSI-14 at 74.76 and RSI-21 at 81.52 are overbought, confirming the trend is extended rather than coiling for a fresh breakout. ADX at 54.67 confirms a strong established trend — not the regime-change consolidation this strategy targets. A breakout setup will re-emerge if price consolidates for several more sessions between $208–$215, BB width contracts meaningfully, and then volume surges above 1.5x on a close beyond either boundary.

Four of five agents are bullish on NVDA; the fifth (swing_breakout) is neutral, not bearish. The bullish thesis is consistent across independent frameworks: (1) trend_momentum confirms the strongest ADX reading in recent history (54.67) with full daily EMA alignment and accelerating ROC across all windows; (2) mean_reversion's Branch B fires weakly — the Fib 38.2% retracement at $209.70 and volume-confirmed hourly support at $208.20 provide confluence for a dip-buy in a strong trend, even though Branch A (fade the extreme) does not fire cleanly; (3) catalyst_news identifies an active, in-window catalyst — hyperscaler AI capex disclosures from Alphabet, MSFT, AMZN, and META are live and each confirmed GPU spend increment is a direct demand signal for NVDA's ~81% AI chip market share; (4) macro_context confirms AI mega-cap is the near-term bull engine in a fragile but risk-on regime, with an asymmetric 5.5:1 R/R at current entry.

Market context weighting: This is a strong-trend, catalyst-driven environment — swing_trend_momentum and swing_catalyst_news receive elevated weight. The macro regime is transitional (not clean risk-on), so swing_macro_context's near-risk-off tail risk (US-Iran, Warsh uncertainty) is noted but does not rise to veto level given AI sector outperformance continuity.

Entry is highly consensual at $209.25 (4/5 agents identical). Stop consensus: median of $204.50, $205.30, $205.30, $205.30, $206.50 = $205.30 (three agents agree exactly). Target requires resolution: mean_reversion targets $216.83 (near-term retest), catalyst_news targets $221.89 (1.272 hourly Fib ext), breakout targets $223.43 (conditional on breakout above $216.83, not active), and trend_momentum + macro_context both target $231.13 (1.272 daily Fib extension). The median of the four active (non-conditional) targets is approximately $221.89. The cluster is $216-224 near-term and $231 full-trend. Synthesized target: $222.50, representing the center of the near-term cluster and deferring the $231 target to trend continuation after the $216.83 resistance is cleared.

Confidence is capped at 72 (not the 78 of trend_momentum) due to: elevated RSI-14 at 74.76 and RSI-21 at 81.52; z-score at 1.92 near overextension; hourly OBV declining and hourly MACD slightly negative indicating short-term distribution within the consolidation; and mean_reversion firing only weakly (not a high-conviction dip-buy confirmation). The setup is real and the directional call is clear, but entry timing sensitivity is high — a break below $208.20 before a new high would invalidate the dip-buy thesis and shift back to wait.
NVDA.key_conflicts: The primary conflict is target depth: swing_mean_reversion targets only $216.83 (a retest of the recent swing high, ~3.6% upside from entry), while swing_trend_momentum and swing_macro_context target $231.13 (~10.4% upside), with swing_catalyst_news at $221.89 in the middle. This divergence is informative — mean_reversion is signaling that the extension is real and the dip-buy is only a near-term bounce trade, not a fresh impulse leg. The resolution: use $222.50 as a provisional target and re-evaluate at $216.83 resistance; if price clears $216.83 on expanding volume the $231 target activates. Secondary conflict: swing_breakout is neutral while the other four are bullish. This is not a genuine disagreement — breakout correctly identifies that the prior squeeze-fired breakout is complete and a new volatility expansion setup has not yet formed. The breakout agent's neutral signal means 'no breakout setup today,' not 'this stock is going down.' A third, lower-weight conflict: mean_reversion's stop at $205.30 and catalyst_news's stop at $206.50 are slightly looser than trend_momentum's $204.50. The $205.30 level (hourly 61.8% Fib retracement) is the most structurally justified stop — it gives the $208.20 support zone room to hold while invalidating the trade on a deeper breakdown.

NVDA.reasoning: TREND: Full daily EMA uptrend confirmed — EMA_10 (204.98) > EMA_21 (197.47) > EMA_50 (190.15) — with ADX_14 at 54.67 (up from 49.47 per prior wiki read), +DI 39.03 vs -DI 4.66. Trend is not just intact, it is strengthening. Higher highs and higher lows preserved in recent price action (Apr 23 low 197.22 → Apr 27 high 216.83 → current mild pullback). MOMENTUM: Daily ROC is positive and expanding across all three lookback windows: 5d +3.33%, 10d +5.22%, 21d +26.69% — textbook momentum acceleration. Daily MACD histogram +1.80 with MACD line above signal — momentum intact on the daily. ENTRY ZONE: Price at $209.25 is sitting just below EMA_5 (209.28) and 2.1% above EMA_10 (204.98), effectively testing the short-term pullback zone the wiki anticipated ($204–$208). Today's intraday low of $207.58 touched that zone. This is a valid pullback-to-EMA setup with trend and daily momentum both clean. HOURLY DIVERGENCE (early warning, not disqualifying): Hourly ROC 5d/10d both -0.71%, hourly MACD histogram -0.59, hourly -DI (27.82) slightly above +DI (25.43) — the intraday timeframe is in a short consolidation/pullback. This is consistent with the daily setup: price digesting the April 23–27 rip before continuation. Hourly EMA alignment remains uptrend (ema_24 > ema_48 > ema_120). RISKS: RSI_14 at 74.76 and RSI_21 at 81.52 are elevated; daily z-score 1.92; volume has been below average last two sessions (0.84 and relative declining). Extended readings cap confidence but do not negate a trend-continuation setup when ADX is this strong and ROC is accelerating. WIKI: Prior read (ADX 49.47, ROC +27%, pullback zone $204–$208) is confirmed and slightly upgraded by today's data (ADX 54.67). No contradiction — current data strengthens the bull case. MACRO: Macro regime is risk-on, S&P best month since 2020 (+9.3% April), AI mega-cap earnings are the near-term bull engine — directly tailwinds NVDA. Stop set below EMA_10; target at Fib 1.272 extension.

1. MACRO REGIME: Transitional, leaning risk-on. S&P 500 up +9.3% in April (best month since 2020), powered by AI mega-cap earnings (Alphabet +7%, Amazon +4%). Fed held at 4.25%-4.50% with zero cuts now priced through 2026 and into 2027 (higher-for-longer). Active tail risks: US-Iran tensions driving oil elevated, Warsh succession uncertainty. The regime is fragile but not risk-off — AI tailwind is the near-term bull engine. 2. RELATIVE STRENGTH: NVDA is a clear sector leader. Daily ROC: +3.33% (5d), +5.22% (10d), +26.69% (21d) vs. broad market +9.3% for the full month — NVDA has nearly tripled the market's pace. Full EMA alignment uptrend confirmed daily and hourly. ADX 54.67 signals a very strong trend. Price holds above all major MAs (5/10/20/50/200 EMA and SMA). This is unambiguous outperformance — AI chip sector is the leading sector. 3. ASYMMETRIC R/R: Entry ~$209.25 (current price, consolidating from $216.83 high). Target: $231.13 (daily Fib 1.272 extension from the $164.27-$216.83 swing) = +10.4% upside. Stop: $205.30 (hourly Fib 0.618 retracement, below the volume-confirmed $208.20 support zone) = -1.9% downside. R/R ratio = 10.4% / 1.9% = ~5.5:1, well above the preferred 3:1 minimum. 4. CAPITAL PRESERVATION: NVDA thesis shows near-zero leverage, 61% revenue growth, 144% earnings growth, 59% operating margin — balance sheet is not a liability even if conditions tighten. Only flags: RSI-14 at 74.76 and RSI-21 at 81.52 are overbought readings; z-score vs 50-day is 1.92 (elevated); hourly OBV trend is down and hourly MACD histogram is negative, indicating short-term distribution pressure. This is consolidation, not reversal. Concern: at +26.69% in 21 days, NVDA may be maturing in the sector rotation cycle rather than early-stage, but the multi-year AI capex supercycle thesis extends this window well beyond typical 2-6 week rotation. Macro regime continuity confirmed vs. wiki state. Wiki thesis explicitly cautions against chasing — entry at the current pullback to hourly Fib 0.382 aligns with disciplined entry protocol.

Branch B fires weakly; Branch A does not cleanly fire. BRANCH A (Fade the Extreme): Daily RSI(21)=81.52 and RSI(14)=74.76 are elevated, but z-score at 1.92 falls just short of the +2.0 threshold. Bollinger %B=0.7824 places price inside the bands (upper band at $220.24 vs current $209.25), so no BB extreme. No RSI divergence detected. Hourly RSI=51.94 is neutral — the daily/hourly extreme divergence required for a high-conviction fade is absent. ADX=54.67 with plus_DI=39.03 vs minus_DI=4.66 signals an exceptionally strong trend, making a fade especially dangerous. Wiki context confirms the prior z-score was 2.39 (higher) and has since cooled to 1.92, suggesting the extension already absorbed some selling — a repeat fade at a lower reading is lower-conviction. Branch A: does not fire. BRANCH B (Buy the Dip in Trend): Full daily EMA alignment confirmed (ema_aligned_uptrend=true), ADX=54.67 — trend structure is unambiguous. NVDA pulled back from the 216.83 swing high to 209.25 over three sessions. On the hourly Fibonacci (swing high 216.82 / swing low 198.19), the 38.2% retracement sits at $209.70 — current price $209.25 is testing this level. The hourly pivot low at $208.20 (15 tests, volume-confirmed) provides horizontal support confluence with the 38.2% Fib, a textbook Branch B confluence zone. Hourly volume bias is 'down' (relative volume 0.56) — healthy declining volume on the pullback, not a distribution signal. The wiki constructive zone is $204–$208, so the ideal entry is marginally lower; hourly RSI=51.94 has not reached the <35 oversold confirmation target, reducing conviction. However, the Fib+support confluence at $208.20–$209.70 is real. Branch B fires weakly. RISK NOTES: The 3-day pullback from $216.83 is still shallow (only to the 38.2% level); if $208.20 support breaks, the 50% retracement at $207.50 and 61.8% at $205.30 are next logical supports. Stop placed below the 61.8% hourly Fib ($205.30) to give the setup room without excessive drawdown. Target is retest of the $216.83 recent swing high. Risk/reward: entry $209.25, target $216.83 (+3.6%), stop $205.30 (-1.9%), RR ~1.9:1 — acceptable but not exceptional for a mean reversion setup.

CATALYST WINDOW: Hyperscaler AI capex disclosures (Alphabet, MSFT, AMZN, META earnings week of Apr 28, currently live) are the primary catalyst — each confirmed incremental GPU spend is a direct demand signal for NVDA as the ~81% AI chip market share leader. This catalyst is in-window NOW (Apr 30, 2026). Secondary catalyst: NVDA own earnings ~May 27 AMC (~18 trading days out, outside entry window but sets a hard exit deadline of ~May 17 per the 3-day pre-earnings blackout). Fed rate decision today (Apr 30) widely expected hold — low impact, already priced.

NET NEWS SENTIMENT: Positive. NVDA hit a 52-week high of $216.83 on Apr 28, up 27% in 21 trading days. Intel Q1 2026 blowout ($13.58B vs $12.42B est.) and AMD record data center revenue both confirm AI hardware demand acceleration. Blackwell Ultra leads market by two generations; Rubin architecture on deck for late 2026. LG humanoid/AI partnership (Apr 29) expands customer base. Negatives: H20 China export ban = $4.5B Q1 charge (already absorbed, priced in); Alphabet TPU long-term competitive risk (not near-term); OpenAI revenue miss (Apr 28) creates mild uncertainty but hyperscaler capex is a separate dynamic.

INSIDER ACTIVITY: Quiet in current 30-day window (no recent trades in data). Wiki notes consistent insider selling trend (CFO and board member sold at $171-177 in March 2026 on likely 10b5-1 plans — well below current $209 price). Not an acute near-term signal; treat as background bearish lean.

TECHNICAL SETUP QUALITY: Constructive. All daily EMAs aligned uptrend. Price at $209.25 has pulled back ~3.5% from the $216.83 high on declining volume (relative volume 0.84 vs 20-day avg), suggesting a normal post-breakout consolidation rather than distribution. Daily ADX at 54.67 confirms strong trend. Daily RSI-14 at 74.76 / RSI-21 at 81.52 are elevated (overbought caution flag), but given the 26.69% ROC over 21 days and catalyst momentum this is not disqualifying. MACD histogram positive (+1.80) and expanding. Hourly RSI-21 at 51.94 — neutral/balanced intraday, not exhausted. Hourly MACD slightly negative (-0.59) but within normal consolidation range. Hourly volume bias 10d is down and OBV down on hourly — some informed distribution concern, but daily OBV trend remains up with no price divergence. Key entry support: $208.20 (volume-confirmed hourly pivot, 15 tests). Pre-catalyst daily support: $179.17 (15 tests). Nearest resistance: $214.73 (hourly), then $216.83 (recent high). Fib ext targets: $221.89 (1.272) and $228.34 (1.618) from the hourly swing.

combined_analysis.signal_determination: Neutral based on weighted signal comparison
